rawContent: {"stage":"review","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Accepting studentId from request body allows any authenticated student to impersonate another student — always use user.id from the authenticated session instead","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"A check-then-insert pattern (find duplicate, then create) has a race condition window where concurrent requests can both pass the check and insert duplicates","scope":"src/app/api/assignments/[id]/submit"},{"kind":"convention","text":"Use withAuth middleware to gate endpoints and extract user.id from the authenticated context — never trust client-supplied identity fields","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Duplicate submission protection must use a database constraint or transaction, not a find-then-insert in two separate calls","scope":"data integrity"},{"kind":"decision","text":"The reference pattern for quiz submission (findByID validation + {success, data} response shape) is correct and should be replicated, but auth must
